title: Join
text:
We’re open to highly motivated collaborators who want to build useful, clinically grounded stroke research and neuroAI.
Good fits - Undergrad / med students who can commit consistently (data + writing) - Residents/fellows interested in outcomes/epidemiology or imaging/AI - Research engineers / data scientists (Python, EHR data, NLP, imaging)
What to email Send CV + 2–3 sentences describing your interests and what you want to learn/build. If you have it, include a short writing sample or GitHub/code snippet.

title: People
text:
Principal Investigator




Liqi Shu, MD
Interventional Vascular Neurology (Stroke Institute Fellow) • UPMC Department of Neurology (Pittsburgh, PA)
Instructor of Neurology (UPMC) • Adjunct Faculty of Neurology (Brown University)

title: People
text:
Key collaborators


 Rebecca Hubbard, PhD
Brown University • Carl Kawaja and Wendy Holcombe Professor of Public Health; Professor of Biostatistics & Data Science
Develops statistical methods for real-world EHR/claims data to improve validity under measurement error, missingness, and complex observation processes.




 Jonghwan Lee, PhD
Brown University • Associate Professor of Engineering; Associate Professor of Brain Science
Leads work at the intersection of biomedical optics/photonics, neural engineering, and AI for next-generation imaging and neurotechnology.





 Zhicheng Jiao, PhD
Brown University • Director, Brown Radiology AI Lab & Brown Imaging Core
Builds medical image analysis and computer vision methods with a focus on robust, clinically deployable imaging pipelines.



 Tiffany Xiao, PhD
Economics, UC Santa Barbara Collaborates on study design and causal inference for real-world evidence questions in stroke and outcomes research.

title: Publications
text:
Full list
For an always-updated complete full list, see Google Scholar.

title: Shu Lab
text:
We build clinically grounded, data-driven tools to improve stroke prevention, neuroendovascular care, and neurorecovery—connecting large-scale EHR evidence, imaging, and translational insights to decisions that matter at the bedside.
Our work spans national administrative datasets (e.g., NIS/NRD), multicenter registries, and imaging/AI pipelines, with a bias toward deployable clinical utility (risk stratification, prognostication, and workflow tools).
:::

title: Shu Lab
text:
Focus areas



Peri-procedural stroke
Risk stratification and prevention around procedures and EVT workflows.



Cerebrovascular epidemiology
Nationwide and multicenter evidence (NIS/NRD, registries).



Imaging + computer vision
Neuroimaging phenotypes and practical AI pipelines.



Neurorecovery analytics
Kinematics and neuroanatomy-informed recovery models.

title: Shu Lab
text:

title: Research
text: We study perioperative/periprocedural stroke risk, mechanisms, and prevention strategies, aiming for tools that can inform counseling, peri-procedural management, and early detection.

title: Research
text: We study perioperative/periprocedural stroke risk, mechanisms, and prevention strategies, aiming for tools that can inform counseling, peri-procedural management, and early detection.

title: Research
section: Cervical artery dissection (CeAD): acute treatment and recurrence
text:
Cervical artery dissection (CeAD): acute treatment and recurrence
Large-scale analyses of CeAD incidence, outcomes, and recurrence, including nationwide work on IV thrombolysis in CeAD-related AIS and longitudinal incidence trends.

title: Research
section: Cerebral venous thrombosis (CVT): prediction and long-term outcomes
text:
Cerebral venous thrombosis (CVT): prediction and long-term outcomes
Risk stratification after CVT, including prediction of post-CVT epilepsy and recurrent venous thrombosis using multicenter registries and pragmatic clinical scores.

title: Research
section: Imaging + AI (medical image analysis & computer vision)
text:
Imaging + AI (medical image analysis & computer vision)
We collaborate on radiology and imaging-core efforts to build robust analysis pipelines for neuroimaging and computer vision applications—prioritizing clinically useful outputs.

title: Research
text:
Neurorecovery & rehabilitation analytics
In partnership with neurorecovery groups, we analyze post-stroke upper extremity kinematics and structural neuroimaging to connect neuroanatomy with functional recovery and rehabilitation outcomes.
